svg.text: The work of holding resources, standards, and intelligence on behalf of the public health field. PHIs receive federal dollars and pass them through to
local actors, maintain accreditation and quality systems the field depends on, and curate field-level data, because they are trusted intermediaries
who serve the field's interest rather than their own.
Clean fiscal stewardship and accreditation build
administrative trustworthiness, so funders route money
through the PHI rather than direct. One compliance failure
A nurtured workforce accumulates know-how, codification
makes it durable, durable capability builds trust and wins
funding, financial stability pays to keep the people.
The compliance machine produces the clean audits that
build trust, which wins funding, which pays for the
compliance machine. A small loop nested inside R1.
Workforce departs, tacit knowledge drains (only codified
survives), effectiveness and trust slip, funding falls,
retention worsens, more leave. R2 in reverse; codification
The reserve funds the compliance machine, but indirect
rates rarely cover its true cost, so it is a net drain on
financial stability. An indirect-rate cap deepens it.

text.tspan: Funders rent trustworthiness. One compliance failure spends years of it.
Codification is the lever that lets the knowledge compound instead of resetting.
Compliance partly pays for itself by producing credibility. Nested inside R1.
R2 in reverse. The workforce cliff flips it; codification limits the loss.
The machine that produces clean audits is structurally underfunded.

g.text: indirect rates fall short → net drain (−)

svg.text: the control point that limits B1
codified knowledge. In practice: succession, mentorship, docs.
It is the single control on how much of the workforce cliff becomes
permanent loss. And because tacit knowledge also runs the compliance
machine, the brain drain (B1) and compliance trap (B2) are coupled
